Full Stack Engineer.jd:
# Full Stack Engineer - Scalable Web & AI Systems
**Location:** San Francisco, CA - Onsite (Flexible)
**Team:** USAvionix
**Type:** Full-time

We’re building a new generation of autonomous ISR aircraft capable of sensing, deciding, and acting in milliseconds, without relying on the cloud.

We’re looking for a Full Stack Engineer to help design and scale the web and backend systems powering our AI and autonomy platforms. You’ll build the real-time infrastructure that connects aircraft, AI agents, and mission control, with performance and reliability at the core.

### **What you’ll do**
- Build full-stack applications using Next.js, React, Node.js, and TypeScript.
- Design scalable data models and APIs using MongoDB, PostgreSQL, and Redis.
- Develop distributed workflows that manage multiple AI agents in real time.
- Integrate internal tools and real-time telemetry pipelines.
- Collaborate with autonomy, AI, and hardware teams to ensure seamless system interaction.

### **What we’re looking for**
- Strong background in Next.js, React, Node.js, and TypeScript.
- Experience with MongoDB, PostgreSQL, and distributed systems.
- Understanding of scalable architectures, caching, and data replication.
- Familiarity with AI-assisted development tools (Cursor, Copilot).
- Independent engineer comfortable operating in a high-autonomy environment.

## **Why USAVX**
USAvionix builds autonomous systems that act faster and smarter than the threat. In this role, you’ll own one of our core technologies - shaping how autonomy is coordinated, deployed, and trusted in the field.

Aerospace Mechanical Engineer.jd:
## Aerospace Mechanical Engineer – Airframe & Prototyping
**Location:** San Francisco, CA – Onsite or Hybrid
 **Team:** Airframe & Mechanical Systems
### About Us
USAvionix is building autonomous aircraft that actually operate in the real world. Our Delta platform moves through rapid prototype cycles, starting with electrically powered, ducted-fan aircraft. This work matters beyond a demo. It matters in mission contexts.
### About The Role
We’re looking for an early-career Aerospace Mechanical Engineer to help design, build, test, and iterate airframes and mechanical subsystems.
 You don’t need prior aircraft experience. What we value most is **strong fundamentals, CAD ability, curiosity, and the drive to turn ideas into hardware that flies**.
This role is hands-on. You’ll be involved in CAD, prototyping, test setups, and flight support.
### What You’ll Do
- Design and iterate mechanical and airframe components for electrically powered autonomous aircraft
- Create and maintain CAD models and assemblies
- Build prototypes with 3D printing, composites, and basic machining
- Support ground testing and flight test preparation
- Build test rigs, fixtures, and simple tooling
- Analyze test results and failures and apply learnings to next iterations
- Collaborate with autonomy, avionics, and systems engineers
### What You Should Have
- Degree in Mechanical Engineering, Aerospace Engineering, or equivalent hands-on experience
- Strong mechanical fundamentals and problem solving
- Experience or comfort with CAD (SolidWorks, Fusion 360, or similar)
- Passion for building and testing real hardware
- Ability to learn airframe and flight concepts quickly
- A bias toward action in a fast prototype environment
You don’t need previous aircraft or airframe experience. If you can learn fast and ship parts that work, you’ll thrive here.
### Nice to Have
- UAV, RC, aerospace, or flight project experience
- Experience with 3D printing, composites, CNC
- Exposure to structural analysis or basic FEA
- Test rig or flight test support experience
### Who You Are
You are a doer. You turn CAD into parts. You learn by building and testing. You are not afraid of failure, you learn from it. You want to grow into bigger technical ownership over time.
### Why You’ll Love Working Here
- Meaningful ownership early in your career
- Tight loop from CAD to prototype to flight
- Small, focused team with direct impact
- Opportunity to work across mechanical systems, structures, and flight test

Computer Vision Engineer.jd:
# Computer Vision Engineer - Perception Systems
**Location:** San Francisco, CA - Onsite (Flexible)
**Team:** USAvionix
**Type:** Full-time

We’re building a new generation of autonomous ISR aircraft capable of sensing, deciding, and acting in milliseconds - without relying on the cloud.

You’ll lead the perception stack that powers onboard visual intelligence. Your work will enable aircraft to detect, track, and understand the world in real time.

## What you’ll do
- Build and deploy high-performance visual detection, tracking, and classification systems for real-time flight.
- Integrate perception models with onboard agents (Intel, Pilot, Operator) to drive coordinated autonomy.
- Optimize CNN and transformer-based models for embedded inference (Jetson, Hailo, or equivalent).
- Develop pipelines for training, testing, and validating vision models using aerial and ground datasets.
- Implement real-time sensor fusion across video, IMU, GPS, and telemetry data streams.
- Contribute to continuous improvement of data collection, labeling, and active-learning loops.

## What we're looking for
- Strong background in computer vision, deep learning, and image/video processing.
- Experience with modern vision architectures (YOLOv5/v8, DETR, RT-DETR, or similar).
- Proficiency in Python and PyTorch; familiarity with TensorRT, OpenVINO, or ONNX runtimes.
- Experience deploying optimized models on embedded hardware (NVIDIA Jetson, Hailo, etc.).
- Understanding of multi-sensor fusion and real-time inference under constrained resources.

## Nice to have
- Experience building flight-ready or edge-deployed perception systems.
- Familiarity with PX4/ArduPilot/MAVLink or other UAV telemetry systems.
- Background in optical flow, 3D reconstruction, or SLAM.
- Comfort working with aerial datasets, synthetic data generation, or 3D simulation (Three.js, Unreal, Cesium).
- Knowledge of model compression, quantization, and real-time video encoding/streaming.

## **Why USAVX**
USAvionix builds autonomous systems that act faster and smarter than the threat. In this role, you’ll own one of our core technologies - shaping how autonomy is coordinated, deployed, and trusted in the field.

Talent Intern \u2013 Recruiting & Admini.jd:
### Talent Intern – Recruiting & Administrative Support
**Location:** Tijuana, Mexico (Onsite or Hybrid)
 **Team:** Talent & Company Support
### About Us
USAvionix is building autonomous aircraft designed to operate in real-world missions. We are an early-stage, hands-on team moving quickly across hardware, autonomy, and flight systems. As we grow, we’re building a strong pipeline of engineers and interns who want to work on real systems, not demos.
### About The Role
We’re looking for a Talent Intern to support recruiting and light administrative work across the company.
This role is ideal for someone who enjoys working with people, staying organized, and helping teams move faster. You’ll work closely with the founding team to coordinate candidates, manage follow-ups, and keep recruiting efforts running smoothly.
This is not a corporate HR role. It’s a startup execution role focused on getting things done.
### What You’ll Do
- Coordinate candidates and internship applications
- Review and organize incoming resumes
- Schedule interviews and follow-ups
- Communicate with candidates throughout the process
- Support relationships with universities and internship programs
- Assist with light administrative and coordination tasks as needed
### What You Should Have
- Currently pursuing or recently completed a degree in Psychology, Business, Human Resources, or a related field
- Strong communication and organizational skills
- Comfort working with multiple tasks and priorities
- Interest in recruiting, people coordination, or startup operations
- Reliability and attention to detail
### Nice to Have
- Prior experience in recruiting or HR internships
- Experience working with spreadsheets, Airtable, or similar tools
- Exposure to technical or engineering environments
### Who You Are
You’re organized, proactive, and dependable. You like helping teams move faster and keeping things from falling through the cracks. You’re comfortable working closely with founders and adapting as priorities shift.
### Why You’ll Love Working Here
- Real responsibility and trust as an intern
- Direct exposure to startup recruiting and growth
- Opportunity to work closely with technical teams
- Flexible scope that can grow with performance

Product Manager - Autonomy Systems.jd:
# Product Manager - Autonomy Systems
**Location:** San Francisco, CA - Onsite (Flexible)
**Team:** USAvionix
**Type:** Full-time

We’re building a new generation of autonomous ISR aircraft capable of sensing, deciding, and acting in milliseconds, without relying on the cloud.

You’ll define and drive one of the most advanced autonomy products in development today, shaping how aircraft perceive, reason, and operate as a coordinated system.

## What you’ll do
- Define product strategy and roadmap across hardware, autonomy, and AI systems.
- Translate mission and customer needs into technical requirements and milestones.
- Partner with engineering to balance speed, feasibility, and product quality.
- Lead planning and prioritization across the Delta (aircraft) and Phalanx (AI) programs.
- Communicate progress, risks, and tradeoffs clearly across teams and leadership.
- Work with design and operations to ensure the product experience is cohesive and scalable.

## **What we’re looking for**
- Proven ability to lead complex technical products from concept to deployment.
- Strong organizational and communication skills.
- Experience working across software and hardware disciplines.
- Comfort translating between engineering detail and product goals.
- Self-driven, adaptable, and comfortable working remotely.

## **Nice to have**
- Background in robotics, embedded systems, or AI products.
- Familiarity with tools like Linear, Notion, or GitHub.
- Experience in early-stage or R&D environments.

## **Why USAVX**
USAvionix builds autonomous systems that act faster and smarter than the threat. In this role, you’ll own one of our core technologies - shaping how autonomy is coordinated, deployed, and trusted in the field.

AI Engineer.jd:
# AI Engineer - Edge Autonomy (LLM Focus)
**Location:** San Francisco, CA - Onsite (Flexible)
**Team:** USAvionix
**Type:** Full-time

We’re building a new generation of autonomous ISR aircraft capable of sensing, deciding, and acting in milliseconds, without relying on the cloud.
Your work will power the onboard AI agents that fly, detect, and coordinate missions in real time.

## What you’ll do
- Build and fine-tune LLM systems that power onboard decision-making for autonomous aircraft.
- Design agentic workflows that coordinate three core AI agents onboard:
    - Operator Agent – mission brain, planning and arbitration
    - Pilot Agent – real-time flight and camera control
    - Intel Agent – perception and verification
- Develop low-latency communication protocols between these agents to ensure reliable, deterministic behavior during flight.
- Build prompt orchestration and retrieval pipelines for situational awareness and mission control.
- Deploy optimized models on embedded hardware that handle variable telemetry in real time.
## What we’re looking for
- Strong background in Python, PyTorch, and modern LLM frameworks (e.g., Hugging Face, vLLM, llama.cpp).
- Experience with fine-tuning, RAG, embeddings, or custom model adapters.
- Comfort building from research ideas to production-ready code.
- Experience working with real-time or mission-critical systems.
- Strong understanding of edge inference (quantization, optimization, GPU/CPU/NPU).
## Nice to have
- Familiarity with small language models (1–3B), grammar-constrained decoding, or state-space models.
- PX4/ArduPilot/MAVLink or drone systems.
- Vision systems (OCR, lightweight detectors) or real-time telemetry handling.
- Experience with 3D simulation (Three.js, Cesium, or similar).

These agents don’t live in the cloud, they fly onboard, coordinating in real time to search, verify, and adapt.

## **Why USAVX**
USAvionix builds autonomous systems that act faster and smarter than the threat. In this role, you’ll own one of our core technologies - shaping how autonomy is coordinated, deployed, and trusted in the field.

Mechanical Engineering Intern \u2013 Pro.jd:
### Mechanical Engineering Intern – Prototyping & Test Systems
**Location:** San Francisco, CA – Onsite or Hybrid
 **Team:** Airframe & Mechanical Systems
### About Us
USAvionix is building autonomous aircraft designed to operate in real-world missions. Our Delta platform moves quickly through prototype cycles, combining autonomy, compute, and flight hardware. This work is hands-on, fast-moving, and focused on building systems that actually fly.
### About The Role
We’re looking for a Mechanical Engineering Intern to support prototyping, test setups, and mechanical integration across the Delta platform.
This is a hands-on role. You will design, build, assemble, and iterate physical hardware used for testing and early flight development. You’ll work closely with engineers across autonomy, hardware, and avionics teams, helping turn concepts into real, testable systems.
This role is ideal for someone who learns by building and wants real exposure to aerospace-adjacent hardware development.
### What You’ll Do
- Design and build mechanical test rigs and fixtures
- Create CAD models and assemblies for prototype hardware
- Assemble and integrate mechanical components and housings
- Support ground testing and test setup preparation
- Iterate designs based on test results and feedback
- Help keep prototype hardware organized, accessible, and testable
### What You Should Have
- Currently pursuing or recently completed a degree in Mechanical Engineering, Aerospace Engineering, or similar
- Comfort with CAD tools such as SolidWorks or Fusion 360
- Interest in hands-on prototyping and physical systems
- Willingness to assemble, disassemble, and iterate hardware
- Curiosity and a bias toward action
### Nice to Have
- Experience with 3D printing, light machining, or composites
- Experience building mechanical fixtures or test setups
- UAV, robotics, or hardware project experience
### Who You Are
You like building real things. You care about how parts fit together and how systems are tested. You’re not afraid to get your hands dirty, learn quickly, and improve designs through iteration.
### Why You’ll Love Working Here
- Real ownership as an intern
- Direct exposure to flight hardware and testing
- Tight feedback loop from CAD to physical systems
- Small team with meaningful impact

Hardware / Embedded Systems Intern \u201.jd:
### Hardware / Embedded Systems Intern – AI Test & Integration
**Location:** San Francisco, CA – Onsite or Hybrid
 **Team:** Autonomy, Hardware & AI Systems
### About Us
USAvionix is building autonomous aircraft designed to operate in real-world missions. Our Delta platform combines autonomy, onboard compute, and high-speed flight hardware. We move quickly from idea to test to iteration, with a strong bias toward systems that actually work outside the lab.
### About The Role
We’re looking for a Hardware or Embedded Systems Intern to support AI system bring-up, testing, and integration across the Delta platform.
This is a hands-on role focused on building and validating real systems. You will work with Raspberry Pi based platforms, cameras, and AI accelerators to help bring up test setups for Delta AI and Phalanx AI modules.
You will not be training models or building perception algorithms from scratch. You will be helping make sure the hardware and software stack runs reliably and is easy to test and iterate on.
### What You’ll Do
- Bring up and configure Raspberry Pi based systems
- Integrate cameras and basic capture pipelines
- Build and maintain AI test harnesses and bench setups
- Support testing of Delta AI and Phalanx AI modules
- Document setup steps, configurations, and test procedures
- Assist in debugging basic integration and stability issues
### What You Should Have
- Currently pursuing or recently completed a degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, Mechatronics, Robotics, or similar
- Comfort working with Linux based systems
- Experience or interest in embedded systems or robotics
- Familiarity with Python or C++
- Willingness to debug and learn through hands-on testing
### Nice to Have
- Experience with Raspberry Pi or similar SBCs
- Camera or sensor integration experience
- Exposure to robotics, autonomy, or AI systems
- Experience with basic electronics and wiring
### Who You Are
You like making systems work. You’re comfortable when things are half-built and messy. You enjoy bringing hardware and software together and learning by doing.
### Why You’ll Love Working Here
- Real exposure to AI and autonomous systems
- Hands-on ownership of test and integration work
- Tight feedback loop from bench testing to flight systems
- Opportunity to grow into deeper technical ownership over time

Hardware Engineer.jd:
# Hardware Engineer - Edge Computer Vision Systems
**Location:** San Francisco, CA - Onsite (Flexible)
**Team:** USAvionix
**Type:** Full-time

We are building a new generation of autonomous ISR aircraft capable of sensing, deciding, and acting in milliseconds, entirely onboard and without the cloud.

Your work will bring real-time computer vision to life, from sensor to sky, helping our aircraft perceive, track, and understand the world beneath them.

### **What you’ll do**
- Design and prototype embedded vision systems using Raspberry Pi, Jetson, or ARM-based platforms with CSI or MIPI camera interfaces.
- Implement and optimize real-time computer vision pipelines (Python, OpenCV, TensorRT, ONNX) for detection, tracking, and data capture.
- Debug hardware and firmware issues across cameras, sensors, connectors, and PCBs.
- Work with the AI and autonomy teams to integrate perception systems into the onboard compute stack.
### **What we’re looking for**
- Hands-on experience with embedded systems, sensors, and low-level debugging.
- Familiarity with image sensors, optics, and camera calibration.
- Strong Python or C++ skills for pipeline development and optimization.
- Ability to prototype fast, from breadboard to flight-ready hardware.

### **Nice to have**
- Experience with drones, ROS, PX4, or ArduPilot.
- Familiarity with low-latency video streaming or real-time inference pipelines.
- Experience with PCB design, thermal management, or field testing.

## **Why USAVX**
USAvionix builds autonomous systems that act faster and smarter than the threat. In this role, you’ll own one of our core technologies - shaping how autonomy is coordinated, deployed, and trusted in the field.

Product Owner - Autonomy Systems.jd:
# Product Owner – Autonomy Systems
### Job Description
**Location:** San Francisco, CA. Onsite preferred.
 **Team:** USAvionix
 **Type:** Full-time

We’re building autonomous ISR aircraft capable of sensing, deciding, and acting in milliseconds, without relying on the cloud.
This role is responsible for execution of the autonomy backlog. The Product Owner operates inside the engineering loop and is accountable for forward motion, clarity, and delivery across the Delta and Phalanx programs.

### What you’ll do
- Own execution of the autonomy backlog across hardware, onboard intelligence, and AI orchestration systems.
- Translate mission goals and high-level direction into epics, stories, and acceptance criteria.
- Work closely with engineering on a daily basis to clarify scope and unblock execution.
- Proactively externalize work in progress using lightweight artifacts such as wireframes, flows, notes, and screenshots.
- Maintain continuous visibility into progress without waiting for prompts from the founder or other stakeholders.
- Drive day-to-day prioritization across the Delta (aircraft) and Phalanx (AI) programs.
- Enforce execution-level tradeoffs between performance, cost, and schedule.
- Validate delivered work against acceptance criteria before it is considered complete.
- Coordinate with design to ensure operator workflows and system behavior remain aligned.
- Communicate execution status, risks, and blockers clearly and consistently.

### What we’re looking for
- Engineering background or prior hands-on technical role strongly preferred and weighted heavily.
- Comfortable operating inside engineering discussions and technical tradeoffs.
- Strong systems-level understanding of software and hardware interaction.
- Experience working directly with engineers in execution-heavy environments.
- Demonstrated ability to move work forward independently without waiting for direction.
- Comfortable sharing incomplete work early to drive fast feedback.
- Strong organization and documentation discipline.

### Nice to have
- Background in robotics, aerospace, defense, or embedded systems.
- Familiarity with Linear, Notion, Slack, and GitHub.
- Experience in early-stage, R&D-heavy, or hardware-constrained environments.
- Former engineer, systems lead, or technical operator.

### Why USAVX
USAvionix builds autonomous systems that must operate under real-world constraints. In this role, you’ll work close to the engineers and close to the product, ensuring execution keeps pace with ambition and progress is always visible.

If you prefer waiting for direction, this is not the role.
If you proactively push work forward, it is.
